title: [v3.4] fix build
body:
install.tools target broke a few weeks ago because it was fetching something @latest. Fix to fetch @hardcoded version.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v3.4/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

`go install` told me to use `go get`. `go get` fails with:
```
go: cannot use path@version syntax in GOPATH mode
```
Unless someone tells me how to solve this, I'm just going to delete the entire `gitvalidation` step on this branch.

@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL. I went with the nuke-from-orbit approach. This branch no longer runs `.gitvalidation`. If this passes muster, I will cherry-pick onto all the rest of the branches.

Oh: context: [nightly cron jobs have been failing on all branches for the last two weeks](https://lists.podman.io/archives/list/podman-monitor@lists.podman.io/thread/3MGM7OFLFG54WWUIMUHZXHSK34PHIFZV/)

LGTM assuming the goal is just to have a working branch.

For a proper fix we would have to backport https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/14098

Ah, thank you, that's the fix I was looking for. It's a little more intimidating than I'd expected, but (per the `go 1.16` line) should maybe work with ancient branches. Is that worth backporting? If so, to all ancient branches, or only a subset?

>  Is that worth backporting? If so, to all ancient branches, or only a subset?

I don't think it is worth it. It likely has different version than what is used on the branches so it cause new failures. Looks like it only went into v4.2 so it may be worth to at least try to get it into the v4.X branches.

@edsantiago: only [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) org members may request cherry picks. You can still do the cherry-pick manually.

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/15860#issuecomment-1253741567):
